name: listDirectory
description: List files and directories in a given path.
examples: Show me what's inside the docs directory
Enumerate the contents of the current folder
whenToUse: The user supplies a directory path and wants to see its immediate children.
There is no file-name pattern to match — just a plain listing of entries.
The user wants names (paths, timestamps, metadata), not file contents.
whenNotToUse: The user wants to match files by name pattern. Use glob instead.
The user wants file contents or token-level payloads. Use scanDirectory or readFile instead.
The user wants to move or rename the directory. Use moveDirectory / renameDirectory instead.

The user wants a recursive directory tree or the full nested structure of a directory (e.g. "the directory structure recursively"). Use scanDirectory instead — listDirectory only shows immediate children.
